title: eriogonum caespitosum var. acaule
intro: Eriogonum caespitosum var. acaule, also known as the dwarf buckwheat or dwarf sulphur-flower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the buckwheat family and is native to the western regions of North America. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and wounds, due to its high concentration of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. The health benefits of Eriogonum caespitosum var. acaule include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea or by making a salve or ointment to apply topically.
